title: The Single Sentence That Names the Woman
subtitle: A journal prompt set for sitting with the full truth of what 84 days of money work has built, and who you are on the other side of it
excerpt: A journal prompt set from the Day 84 theme of a 12-week money programme. The prompts ask not what you have achieved but who you have become — and end with a single sentence written in your own words that names her.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 84 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use at the close of a structured money programme, or any time you want to identify what has genuinely shifted in your identity rather than just your behaviour.

content.text: A journal prompt set from the Day 84 theme of a 12-week money programme. Day 84: anchor abundance as who I am forever. The prompts move from what would shift if abundance were identity rather than practice, toward the single sentence that names who you have become.
New to journal prompts as practice? Read
If abundance were no longer something I attract but something I am, what would shift in my daily life?
Write the practical changes: how you walk, speak, decide, spend.
What would it feel like to know I can never be separate from abundance again?
Write whatever comes, including resistance if it is there.
What truth, moment, or memory will always remind me that abundance lives within me?
Name it precisely. Write what it proves.
How would I walk, speak, create, and love from the truth that I am abundance?
Write each one: walk, speak, create, love. One sentence each.
Write the single sentence that names the woman you have become through this work.
Start with: I am a woman who... Write until the sentence is true.
Adapted from the Day 84 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
